_comment: Secondary (scatter + leakage) radiation data. For diagnostic rooms, NCRP 147 uses an unshielded secondary air kerma PER PATIENT at 1 m (Table 4.7); behind a barrier, K_sec = K1_sec * N_tot / d_sec^2 * B_sec(x), compared with P/T. For CT, the secondary dose is derived from DLP. Sources: NCRP147, BIR2012. All values editable.

diagnostic_secondary_kerma._comment: NCRP 147 Table 4.7: unshielded secondary air kerma K1_sec (mGy per patient at 1 m) for each workload distribution and scatter geometry. 'leak_side_scatter' for lateral barriers; 'forward_back' and 'leak_forward_back' (the conservative total) for barriers in the forward/backward beam directions. Wnorm in mA*min per patient, F = primary field area (cm2) used in the survey.
diagnostic_secondary_kerma._note_bottom_rows: Rows 6-9 (chest, mammography, cardiac, peripheral angiography) read from the rotated table tail; cardiac/mammography values flagged for confirmation against the PDF. Rows 1-5 are high-confidence.

generic_kvp_scatter_fraction._comment: Scatter fraction a1 = scattered air kerma at 1 m from the patient, per unit incident air kerma at 1 m, for a 400 cm2 field at ~90 deg. Used by the transparent first-principles secondary calc for a user-specified kVp (scales with field area / 400). Representative diagnostic values; see NCRP 147 Section 4 / Simpkin.
generic_kvp_scatter_fraction._note: Order-of-magnitude scatter fractions (~10^-3 of incident kerma at 1 m for a 400 cm2 field). For rigorous diagnostic design use the NCRP 147 per-patient secondary kerma table above; this generic set supports the 'custom kVp / any geometry' path and is editable.

ct._comment: CT is a secondary-radiation (scatter) source for shielding. NCRP 147 Section 5.5 derives the scattered air kerma from the dose-length product (DLP). K_sec(d) per week = kappa * DLP_total_per_week / d^2, with kappa the scatter air kerma at 1 m per unit DLP. CT default DLP values are NCRP 147 Table 5.2; replace with your scanner's values or the Saudi SFDA NDRLs (see workloads.json).

body_average.note: average chest/abdomen/pelvis when procedure-specific data unavailable

scatter_air_kerma_per_DLP.units: mGy at 1 m per (mGy*cm of DLP)
scatter_air_kerma_per_DLP.ref: NCRP147 Section 5.5 (representative); confirm with scanner isodose/scatter map
scatter_air_kerma_per_DLP.note: Scattered air kerma at 1 m per unit DLP. Manufacturer isodose data are preferred; this representative value is editable. Some references quote a head/body split - add scanner-specific values here.

tertiary_ceiling_scatter._comment: BIR Chapter 2 method (user-provided excerpt): tertiary scatter off the ceiling/walls reaching an operator depends on ceiling height and barrier height. Scatter air kerma per patient quantity (KAP for interventional, DLP for CT). Significant for high-workload CT and interventional suites. Stored qualitatively; the engine flags when tertiary scatter should be evaluated.
tertiary_ceiling_scatter.note: Dose strongly depends on ceiling height (3-5 m typical) and the height of the protective barrier; ~a few percent of direct exposure but can matter at high workload. Use Figure 2.7 / Table 2.5 of BIR (2012) for the m, C constants per ceiling height; entered by the user for a specific room.
